channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Distinct Patterns of Mobility Recovery After Stroke Using Routine Clinical Data
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 750 adults with stroke identified distinct mobility recovery trajectories during the first 180 days post-stroke using Growth Mixture Modeling based on Activity Measure for Post-Acute Care (AM-PAC) Basic Mobility scores.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis will evaluate the effectiveness of combined biopsychosocial interventions compared to usual care, waiting-list controls, and active unimodal interventions in adults with chronic low back pain, aiming to reduce pain and functional disability.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis will compare the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches on objective and subjective outcomes in individuals with shoulder adhesive capsulitis, based on English-language RCTs published since 2000.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study ... # Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study ... .whatsapp.com/send?text=Early%2 ... Weight-Bearing ... Speeds%20Recovery%2 ... After%20Unstable%20Ankle%20Fracture ... 20Surgery ... 20study%20https%3A%2F%2Fmedicaldialogues.in%2Forthopaedics%2Fearly-weight-bearing-speeds-recovery-after-unstable-ankle-fracture-surgery-study-174806%20
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Association of combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation with post-stroke dementia: a U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study.
item.description: A U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study of 479 propensity score-matched patients found that combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation was associated with a reduced risk of post-stroke dementia compared to physical rehabilitation alone in adults aged ≥55 years who developed MCI within 6 months after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials investigated the effectiveness of various physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis (EDSS score ≤6.0), concluding that while exercise may be beneficial, specific effects remain unclear due to diverse interventions and outcome measures.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Virtual Reality-assisted Physiotherapeutic Training for Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 5 RCTs found that virtual reality (VR)-assisted physiotherapy significantly improved pain (SMD = 0.40, 95% CI: 0.14-0.65, P = 0.002), physical function (SMD = 0.37, 95% CI: 0.05-0.70, P = 0.003), and balance (SMD = 0.44, 95% CI: 0.16-0.72, P = 0.002) in patients with knee osteoarthritis compared to traditional rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Oxford visual perception screen detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors: an early cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study.
item.description: A prospective cross-sectional study among sub-acute adult stroke survivors (within 6 weeks post-stroke) in the UK demonstrated that the Oxford Visual Perception Screen (OxVPS) effectively detects visual perception difficulties, with early-phase diagnostic accuracy assessed using ROC analysis and random forest analysis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries found that the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model did not significantly affect the proportion of hospitals delivering lower extremity joint replacement prehabilitation in CJR treatment areas compared to control areas.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial with 140 TIA and mild stroke participants found that a once-weekly 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved functional exercise capacity (6-minute walk test distance), physical activity, and quality of life over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: This cross-sectional study investigated whether combining gait, balance, and infrared thermography data enhances machine-learning classification between individuals with knee osteoarthritis (KOA) and healthy controls compared with single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: APTA, Hartford HealthCare Launch Frailty Pilot to Advance Value-Based Care
item.description: APTA and Hartford HealthCare Rehabilitation Network launched a pilot to test an early frailty identification and intervention model, positioning physical therapists at the forefront of prevention-first care for aging populations and advancing value-based care.
item.author: APTA

item.title: The association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis confirmed a significant association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain, highlighting the comorbid prevalence and longitudinal relationship between these two conditions in adult populations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] From targets to minutes: making movement advice actionable
item.description: This correspondence suggests shifting physical activity messaging from aspirational thresholds to achievable marginal gains, showing that small increases in activity among the least active can yield the greatest population benefit, aligning with WHO guidance.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: A Multimodal Assessment of Gastric Motility, reflux risk, and Clinical Efficacy: Intermittent Oro-Esophageal versus Nasogastric Tube Feeding for Post-Stroke Dysphagia in a Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A single-center RCT (n=54) found that intermittent oro-esophageal tube feeding for 14 days improved gastric motility and swallowing recovery, and reduced reflux risk compared to continuous nasogastric tube feeding in patients with post-stroke dysphagia.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blinded, sham-controlled trial with 49 participants found that ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (PENS) applied to the radial nerve, combined with exercise, provided mid- and long-term benefits for chronic lateral elbow pain, improving pain intensity, disability, and psychological outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effects of resistance exercise with blood flow restriction on muscle performance, muscle mass, and function in older adults: a systematic review | European Review of Aging and Physical Activity
item.description: This systematic review evaluated the effects of resistance exercise with blood flow restriction (BFR-RE) on muscle performance, muscle mass, and function in older adults, identifying it as a promising strategy for those unable to perform high-intensity resistance exercise.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Frailty assessment and effect of physical activity interventions in older adults: a scoping review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression
item.description: This scoping review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression evaluated frailty assessment and the effect of physical activity interventions in older adults, noting physical activity as a promising strategy to mitigate frailty despite heterogeneity in definitions and assessments.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Assessment and Management Practices for Post-Stroke Shoulder Pain Among Rehabilitation Professionals in Vietnam: A Cross-Sectional Survey.
item.description: A cross-sectional survey investigated assessment and management practices for post-stroke shoulder pain among rehabilitation professionals in Vietnam to identify evidence and practice gaps and barriers to effective care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: A scoping review of outcome and outcome measure sets for adults with spinal cord injury, stroke, and traumatic brain injury.
item.description: A scoping review will synthesize outcome domains and measures included in core outcome sets and standard outcome measure sets for adults with spinal cord injury, stroke, and traumatic brain injury, also examining the involvement of persons with lived experience.
item.author: PubMed
